sample_size.source_quote: Therefore, the total number of patients included in the study was 1984.

mortality_predictors.source_quote: SAPS-3, on ICU admission, had an AUC of 0.75 (95% CI 0.72–0.77).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Six-parameter model (age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin, GCS score, body temperature)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Backward stepwise multivariate logistic regression with ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR > 1 for age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin; OR < 1 for GCS score and body temperature
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin, GCS score, body temperature
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Our six-parameter model had an AUC of 0.72 (95% CI 0.69–0.75) with no difference in predictive strength compared to SAPS-3 (p = 0.071).
SOFA score upon ICU admission had an AUC of 0.67 (95% CI 0.64–0.70). Both SAPS-3 and our model had a better predictive value than the SOFA score (p < 0.001 and p = 0.0019, respectively).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age, immunosuppression, CRP, bilirubin, GCS score, body temperature
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Internal validation of the six-parameter model was undertaken through five-fold cross-validation with 50 repeats, which yielded an AUC of 0.71 (95% CI 0.66–0.76).
